definitions.definition: Alcohols are organic compounds that contain a hydroxyl (-OH) group attached to a carbon atom. They are also known as hydric or hydroxyl compounds. Alcohols are typically derived from carboxylic acids.
definitions.example: Methanol (CH3OH) and ethanol (C2H5OH) are common alcohols.

definitions.definition: Phenol is a type of alcohol that has an aromatic ring and a hydroxyl group (-OH) attached to it. Phenol is a stronger acid than most alcohols due to the presence of the benzene ring.
definitions.example: C6H5OH is the chemical formula for phenol.

definitions.definition: Ethers are organic compounds that contain an ether group (R-O-R') or an alkyl group attached to an oxygen atom. They are typically derived from alcohols.
definitions.example: Dimethyl ether (CH3-O-CH3) and diethyl ether (C2H5-O-C2H5) are common ethers.

definitions.definition: A hydroxyl group is a functional group that consists of a hydrogen atom and a hydroxyl (-OH) group attached to a carbon atom.
definitions.example: The hydroxyl group is essential for the characteristic properties of alcohols and phenols.

definitions.definition: A substituted alcohol is an alcohol that contains a hydroxyl group (-OH) attached to a carbon atom that is also attached to other functional groups or alkyl groups.
A phenolic hydroxyl group is a hydroxyl group (-OH) attached to an aromatic ring.
definitions.example: Phenols contain a phenolic hydroxyl group.

definitions.definition: Esterification is a chemical reaction in which an alcohol reacts with a carboxylic acid to form an ester and water.
definitions.example: Esterification is a common method for synthesizing esters from alcohols and carboxylic acids.

definitions.definition: Esters are organic compounds that contain a carbonyl group (C=O) and an ether group (R-O-R'). They are typically derived from alcohols and carboxylic acids.
definitions.example: Methyl acetate (CH3COOCH3) is a common ester.

definitions.definition: Fischer esterification is a type of esterification reaction in which an alcohol reacts with a carboxylic acid in the presence of an acid catalyst to form an ester and water.
definitions.example: Fischer esterification is commonly used to synthesize esters from alcohols and carboxylic acids.

definitions.definition: Acid-catalyzed esterification is a type of esterification reaction in which an alcohol reacts with a carboxylic acid in the presence of an acid catalyst to form an ester and water.
definitions.example: Acid-catalyzed esterification is commonly used to synthesize esters from alcohols and carboxylic acids.

definitions.definition: Aromatic alcohols are alcohols that contain an aromatic ring and a hydroxyl group (-OH) attached to it.
Alkyl halides are organic compounds that contain a halogen atom (F, Cl, Br, or I) attached to a carbon atom.
Alcohol dehydration is a chemical reaction in which an alcohol loses water to form an alkene or an alkyne.
definitions.example: Alcohol dehydration is commonly used to synthesize alkenes and alkynes from alcohols.

definitions.definition: Oxidation of alcohols is a chemical reaction in which an alcohol is oxidized to form an aldehyde or a ketone.
definitions.example: Oxidation of alcohols is commonly used to synthesize aldehydes and ketones from alcohols.

definitions.definition: Phenol alkylation is a chemical reaction in which a phenol is alkylated with an alkyl halide to form an alkylated phenol.
definitions.example: Phenol alkylation is commonly used to synthesize alkylated phenols from phenol and alkyl halides.

definitions.definition: Ethers can be synthesized from alcohols by treating them with an alkyl halide in the presence of a base.
definitions.example: Ethers from alcohols is commonly used to synthesize ethers from alcohols and alkyl halides.

definitions.definition: Ethers can be synthesized from alkenes by treating them with an alkyl halide in the presence of a base.
definitions.example: Ethers from alkenes is commonly used to synthesize ethers from alkenes and alkyl halides.
